Submitted my application in November before the priority deadline, and paid the application fee. On the day of the priority deadline, they extended the deadline further. They waived the application fee for all applicants who had not submitted their applications and wished to apply later. Due to a system error, my application was not under review, but the portal was showing both under review and awaiting materials status. When I reached out to one of the advisors on May 12, they corrected their error and finally got a rejection letter on 29th May. From these circumstances, I can assume that my application did not get a proper review. Before May, I had reached out to the official email of the College of Arts and Sciences, which was the only communication method for them regarding the application, but I never got any response.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
comment: I already have a recent master's in Writing Studies and Educational Psychology with a 4.0 GPA, as well as over a decade of social work experience, and several academic publications, conference presentations, and workshops that focused on therapeutic techniques, mental wellbeing, or disability. I also taught for my entire master's degree, was an Assistant Director of the Writing Center, and conducted my own 100+ participant study. I was waitlisted on March 30th, told all decisions would be final by April 24th, and then received an email offering me admission into the part-time and non-funded 3-year program (rather than the full-time, funded 2-year program I had applied for) on April 29th. I was told within the next week I would get my official offer of admission, and I had that same one week to accept it and pay the $250 admission fee. I emailed back 6 days later saying I had discussed with my support network and had decided to reject the offer due to national instability and uncertainty of gov financial aid, but would like to reapply in the future. I asked how to indicate this acceptance on a potential future application, as I had never received an official admission offer. Two weeks after that, I got my official admission offer and a host of emails telling me what my next steps were. All of them had inconsistent and contradictory information, including dates that were different email to email and deadlines that had passed weeks ago. One email had three different dates for the same (important) deadline. I finally got a response to the email I had sent directly to the Admissions Office, saying they were sorry to hear I was declining and informing me of the steps I needed to take to officially decline. They did not answer the other questions I had included in my email to them. Throughout all of this, my name was only spelled correctly once. We will see if I do end up reapplying in future years.

Under review with department > Additional Review Required > goes blank for a few hours > Deny. Folks, that's how it works at UIC! US MS - 3.90 & a few publications aren't enough in this brutal season. Priority deadline is a joke. Nowadays I regret studying Mechanical all these years. Worst decision of my life. Advice for young people (especially Internationals) - If you're planning to have a career in the industries, go for ISE, Automotive, Civil, Electrical etc. If enrolled, switch from Mechanical ASAP. Sooner or later, you'll regret picking Mechanical, pretty much anywhere on this planet.

Thank you for your interest in Georgia Tech and for giving us the opportunity to consider your application to the Electrical & Computer Engineering Ph.D. program for the Fall 2026 semester at the Atlanta campus. Your application and supporting materials have been carefully reviewed by the graduate program admissions committee. In evaluating applicants, we consider a combination of factors, including academic degrees and records, essay prompts, letters of recommendation, test scores (if applicable), and relevant work experience. We also consider the alignment of your goals with the graduate program and our academic unit's faculty, as well as the number of openings for the incoming class. We received a large number of highly qualified applicants for the Fall 2026 term. After careful review, we regret to inform you that we are unable to offer you admission at this time. We understand this decision is not what you had hoped for, but we wish you much success as you pursue your academic and professional goals.

comment: Originally applied in January. Admissions sent me an email after April 15 and invited me to a Zoom call where they told me my application would be a better fit in a different program than the one I applied to (PhD Comparative and International Education). They suggested changing my application to EdD in something unrelated to my research statement. I respectfully declined. I expect the portal to show a rejection soon. They could have communicated this decision sooner in my opinion as I had been waiting awhile.

I applied at the end of the application period when the program sent an email saying that there were still positions available. Got ~30 hours notice and no flexibility for a panel interview over Zoom. 5 of the 6 panelists made it very clear that they felt I was wasting their time (6th was youngest, and newest to faculty). Would not recommend this program unless you are dead set on it and can stand up for yourself. I have BScs in chemistry and physics and 8 years experience in various laboratories, was treated with significantly more respect by professors in other programs.

Two weeks past the deadline for a typo-ridden letter and half an email.

Early Nov 2025 contacted potential supervisor and was encouraged to apply, end of Nov 2025 submitted application, mid Jan 2026 submitted application for L4E fellowship. Early Feb 2026 I get a Whatsapp message from my proposed secondary supervisor on a Sunday afternoon (Montreal time) asking if I'm available in the next couple of hours to discuss my application. As an international applicant in a different time zone unfortunately I was already asleep so replied first thing on Monday morning trying to arrange a time we could talk, and was told they would get back to me with a different time. The next thing I hear is on the 21st April when I get sent an email to look at the application portal where I find a generic rejection message. In the mean time I sent numerous follow up messages asking if they still wanted to talk with me but I never got a reply. McGill's website suggested that most applicants would hear back at the end of February and throughout March so towards the end of March I began to reach out by email to the proposed primary supervisor, program administrator, and L4E to see if they could give me any insight into the progress of my application or when I could expect to hear back. These emails and subsequent follow ups were also all completely ignored. I am obviously gutted about my application being rejected however given the seeming distinct lack of compassion, communication, and organisation shown by the anthropology department at McGill and the L4E it raises some major red flags to me about the quality of teaching and support that is offered by both the program and the fellowship. I would be very interested to hear from successful applicants about if this is the case? If the other applications I am still waiting on are also unsuccessful I will be applying for PhD programs again next year, however given the inadequacy of my experience this year I shan't be reapplying to McGill. Very disappointed.
